preview_text:
The model rates Spain as strong favorite, assigning a 80% win probability against an Austria side projected to win in only 2% of simulations. Austria's underdog status is severe; the model sees the match as heavily tilted toward Spain's victory.

The outcome distribution shows Spain's dominance reflected across the full range of scenarios. A draw registers at 18%, representing meaningful but secondary probability—plausible enough that one-in-five replications yield a tie, yet well below Spain's win likelihood. Austria's 2% win probability indicates the model treats an Austrian upset as a tail-risk event rather than a competitive baseline.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model on Spain and the draw, both within 5 percentage points. Sportsbooks assign Austria 7.3% win probability, however—substantially higher than the model's 2.4%, a gap of nearly 5 percentage points. This divergence suggests bookmakers see more viability in an Austrian upset than the statistical forecast does. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment better predicts the match outcome.
